_sources.discrepancies: No major discrepancies found. MA uses Secretary of the Commonwealth (not SoS) as elections authority. NCSL classifies MA as no-document-required for ID. Massachusetts has no-excuse absentee voting and early in-person voting. 2026 primary date (Sept 15) is the standard first Tuesday after second Monday in September per MA law; TODO verify for 2026 cycle. Registration deadline is 10 days before each election per MA law.

earlyVoting.notes: In-person early voting available at local election offices for primaries. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
In-person early voting at local election offices. Hours vary by municipality. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
Massachusetts offers in-person early voting at local election offices. Dates and hours vary by city/town. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Massachusetts does not prohibit phones inside polling places. Voters may not interfere with other voters or officials. Electioneering is prohibited within 150 feet of a polling place entrance.
additionalRules: Semi-closed primary: registered party members vote in their party's primary. Unenrolled (independent) voters may request either party's primary ballot and remain unenrolled afterward.
Massachusetts uses a semi-closed primary system; unenrolled voters choosing a party ballot for the primary are automatically re-enrolled unless they request otherwise at the polls.
No-excuse absentee voting is available for all registered voters.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Noon the day before the election (received by local election office)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the local election office by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the local election office by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Massachusetts has a semi-closed primary (Mass. Gen. Laws c. 53, §37). Registered party members vote in their party's primary. Voters enrolled as 'unenrolled' (independent) may request any party's primary ballot and vote in that primary. After voting, unenrolled voters return to unenrolled status.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Massachusetts tiene una primaria semi-cerrada (Mass. Gen. Laws c. 53, §37). Los miembros registrados en un partido votan en la primaria de su partido. Los votantes inscritos como 'sin inscripción' (independientes) pueden solicitar la boleta de primaria de cualquier partido y votar en esa primaria. Después de votar, los votantes sin inscripción vuelven a ese estatus.
